title: Channel API on the work-stealing thread pool (v1.4 concurrency foundation)
goal: Add a CSP-style channel primitive on top of the existing work-stealing thread pool. Public surface: make-channel, channel-send!, channel-receive!, channel-close!, channel-closed?. Bounded and unbounded variants. Backed by the existing thread pool — sends block when bounded buffer is full; receives block when empty.
next_actions: Define ESHKOL_VALUE_CHANNEL as a new tagged-value subtype (HEAP_SUBTYPE_CHANNEL).

lib/core/runtime_channel.cpp: implement make-channel (capacity), channel-send! (blocking or non-blocking), channel-receive! (blocking or non-blocking, returns either value or the EOF-object on closed), channel-close!.
Use the existing per-thread arena for channel buffer storage; lock-free SPSC ring for capacity=1 channels; mutex+condvar for bounded; concurrent linked queue for unbounded.
Add tests/v1_3_edge_cases/channel_test.esk: producer/consumer over a bounded channel between two threads via parallel-execute; verify FIFO order and back-pressure.
acceptance: Producer / consumer in parallel across 1000 sends maintains FIFO order.
Closed channel: send raises; receive returns EOF object after draining buffer.
Bounded channel with capacity 0 is the synchronous-rendezvous variant (sender blocks until receiver ready).
v1_4_payoff: v1.4 channels are the spec for inter-fiber communication. v1.4 fibers schedule against the same primitive.
